cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
cire: noun. A fabric with a glazed finish.
creep: verb. To move slowly with the abdomen close to the ground.
crepe: noun. A flat round pancake -like pastry from Lower Brittany, made with wheat.
eerie: adjective. Inspiring fear, especially in a mysterious or shadowy way; strange, weird.
epee: noun. A sharp-pointed dueling sword with a bell-shaped guard, used (with the end blunted) in sport fencing.
epic: noun. An extended narrative poem in elevated or dignified language, celebrating the feats of a deity, demigod (heroic epic), other legend
eve: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Even.
ice: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Icelandic.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
pec: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Pesisir.
pee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Taje.
peer: verb. To look with difficulty, or as if searching for something.
per: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Persian.
perceive: verb. To become aware of, through the physical senses, to see; to understand.
peri: noun. A sprite or supernatural being.
pic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pinji.
pice: noun. Alternative form of paisa, especially in the form of coins worth 20 cash.
pie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Piro.
piece: noun. A part of a larger whole, usually in such a form that it is able to be separated from other parts.
piecer: noun. Someone or something that pieces.
pier: noun. A raised platform built from the shore out over water, supported on piles; used to secure, or provide access to shipping; a jetty.
pierce: verb. To puncture; to break through.
pree: verb. to heed, to take notice of or pay attention to
price: noun. The cost required to gain possession of something.
receive: verb. To be given, sent, or paid something.
recipe: noun. A formula for preparing or using a medicine; a prescription; also, a medicine prepared from such instructions.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reeve: noun. Any of several local officials, with varying responsibilities.
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.
rice: noun. Cereal plants, Oryza sativa of the grass family whose seeds are used as food.
rip: verb. To divide or separate the parts of (especially something flimsy, such as paper or fabric), by cutting or tearing; to tear off or out
ripe: adjective. Of a fruit, vegetable, seed, etc., ready for reaping or gathering; having attained perfection; mature.
rive: verb. To tear apart by force; to rend; to split; to cleave.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veep: noun. Abbreviation of Vice President of the United States; the office of Vice President of the United States, especially during an election
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
vice: noun. Bad or immoral behaviour. (Especially often, a habit that harms oneself or others)
vie: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Vietnamese.
vier: noun. Spoken by a player during a match with one or more visually impaired players to indicate the fourth rank in algebraic notation.
viper: noun. A venomous snake in the family Viperidae.
